description:
Looking for the biggest parmi in Brisbane? We think that we have found it - @aromathekitchen.jindalee! Their parmis are huge, and there are heaps of different options for toppings as well, and best of all - they taste amazing! But it's not just parmis here, we loved the ribs and pizza as well. Drinks include smoothies, freshly squeezed juices, freakshakes, mocktails and more, and they're licenced and serve up some amazing cocktails as well. Aroma the Kitchen is in the south-western suburb of Jindalee and open for breakfast, lunch and dinner on the weekends, brunch, lunch and dinner Wednesday to Thursday, and dinner Monday and Tuesday!

@aromathekitchen.jindalee

Ibis PJCC Dining Options include the hotel’s restaurant, Ibis Kitchen, which is spacious and easily accessible for wheelchairs, a significant advantage.

ibis Kitchen maintains the hotel’s minimalist aesthetics and offers private dining areas.

Had an amazing time dining at ibis Kitchen in PJCC! 🍽️✨ The spacious and wheelchair-accessible restaurant keeps the minimalist aesthetics intact while offering private dining areas. The buffet breakfast was a treat with an affordable à la carte menu. 🌟

Breakfast starts at 7 am, featuring a variety of options: cereals, croissants, pastries, salad bar, fruits, congee, hotdogs, scrambled eggs, spicy potatoes, baked beans, fried rice, and noodles. 🍳🥐🍓

We shared fried chicken nuggets, signature nasi goreng, and Spaghetti Aglio e Olio. The nuggets were crispy and juicy, paired perfectly with mayo and chili sauce. 🍗🔥 The nasi goreng was bursting with 'wok hey' flavor and served with homemade Sambal Belacan. However, the Aglio e Olio was a bit too spicy for my taste. 🍝🌶️

Perfect for both business and leisure travelers, ibis PJCC ensures a clean, secure, and affordable stay with the quality assurance of Accor. Whether for work or relaxation, this is a comfortable and practical base. 🏨💼

📍 ibis PJCC Petaling Jaya
📍 Address: 1, Jalan PJS 5/29, Taman Desaria, 46150 Petaling Jaya, Selangor
📞 Phone: 03-7495 7500
🔗 Google Maps

This was the high light of the day. I wanted a store room. Never had one earlier. And I wanted one with open shelving. Basic granite slabs. No frills. 

I had a lot of steel sambadams, given to me for my wedding by my parents, all those are with our production team now. I have a few. 

It’s quite a pain to guess or remember what’s in each dabba, not to mention time consuming. So I decided to invest in glass this time. Clean and easy. And safe ! I still have a few plastic dabbas in the kitchen. Saro said she’ll use those. So over this week, I’ll transfer all into glass. Yes. One will have to be careful. But it’ll be so much easier to see what’s in the dabbas. 

What’s your preference ? Comment and let me know ! 

#mylaporekitchens #storage #storeroom #glasscontainers #containers #cooking #organisation #beingorganised

Easy Vegan Babka inspired by @davidyurman’s Sculpted Cable Collection #ad

Vegan Cinnamon Babka
Yield: 1 loaf (about 8 slices)
Prep Time: 30 minutes
Rise Time:1 hour 15 minutes
Bake Time: 35–40 minutes
Ingredients
Dough
½ cup (120 mL) oat milk, heated to 110°F (43°C)
½ cup (100 g) granulated sugar, divided 3½ cups (440 g) bread flour or all-purpose flour 1 packet (¼ oz / 7 g) active dry yeast ¼ tsp sea salt ½ cup (113 g) vegan butter, at room temperature ½ cup (120 g) unsweetened vegan yogurt, at room temperature 1 tsp vanilla extract 
Cinnamon Filling
½ cup (113 g) vegan butter, cubed
½ cup (120 mL) oat milk ½ cup (100 g) organic brown sugar 3 tsp ground cinnamon 
Vegan Egg Wash
1 tbsp oat milk

Get the full recipe on my sub/stack 🍊

क्या बाथरूम में छोटी-छोटी चीज़ें इधर-उधर बिखरी रहती हैं? तब चाहिए एक स्मार्ट स्टोरेज समाधान! यह Mirror Cabinet आपके बाथरूम को साफ-सुथरा और व्यवस्थित रखने में मदद करता है। इसमें दिए गए कम्पार्टमेंट्स में आप आसानी से अपने साबुन, लोशन, मेकअप आइटम्स, फेस वॉश, शेविंग किट और डेली यूज़ की चीज़ें सहेज सकते हैं।

इसका मिरर फ्रंट आपकी डेली ग्रूमिंग में कमाल का काम करता है और बाथरूम को एक साफ और स्लीक लुक देता है। साथ ही, इसका डिज़ाइन ऐसा है कि इसे लगाने पर दीवार पर ज्यादा जगह भी नहीं घेरता।

मटेरियल ऐसा है जो नमी से जल्दी खराब नहीं होता, इसलिए यह लंबे समय तक चलता है। इंस्टॉल करना भी बहुत आसान है — बस दीवार पर फिट करें और तुरंत इस्तेमाल शुरू!

अगर आप चाहते हैं कि आपका बाथरूम हमेशा साफ, सटीक और थोड़ा सा फैंसी दिखे — तो यह Mirror Cabinet आपके लिए एक सही और फायदेमंद विकल्प है।

Karen’s “Test” Kitchen 🤝🏼 Paleo biscuits 👩🏻‍🍳

Embracing paleo lifestyle is easy but
sometimes the bread cravings are real (pms 🫠). Decided to experiment making a keto biscuit. Not claiming baker status (yet), but I made a blueberry compote muffin and a cheesy chive biscuit with grass-fed @Kerrygoldusa cheddar… they both lowkey slapped 😎. 

Recipe I followed:
2¼ cup almond flour
2½ tsp baking powder 
3/4 tsp sea salt
3 large eggs, room temperature
1/2 cup sour cream, room temperature
6 Tbsp melted butter, save 1/3 of the butter to brush the biscuits before baking. 
3/4 cup (3 oz) shredded sharp cheddar cheese
1 Tbsp minced fresh chives
Mix dry ingredients + mix wet ingredients in a separate bowl, then combine. 
Bake at 425* for 10-12 minutes.
Let 🆒 . Enjoy 😊

Why wait days for dry mint when you can skip the hassle and bake it in no time? Ditch drying it on your counters and join me in this quick and easy method to dehydrate mint! Just bake at 170°F for about 1 hour (depending on how much you’re making), then crush by hand or blend it up. Store in a jar in a cool, dry place, and you’ve got fresh, homemade dried mint ready to go! 🍃✨

墨魚滑紫菜餅🤍𝘾𝙪𝙩𝙩𝙡𝙚𝙛𝙞𝙨𝙝 𝙥𝙖𝙨𝙩𝙚 𝙘𝙖𝙠𝙚

勁香脆🥹好食到一整完即刻俾人食晒！（包括我自己）墨魚滑可以響海鮮店買到～乾身紫菜可以響街市雜貨店都有！Super yummy🖤 煎紫菜的時候唔好太大火，如果唔係燶咗就會有苦味！買到的墨魚滑多數已經有調味，所以都唔使落鹽了☺️

材料: 
墨魚滑 200g
乾紫菜 1塊
芫荽 1扎
蒽 1扎
芝麻 2湯匙
麻油 1湯匙

做法:
1. 紫菜用白鑊細火把兩邊煎至變為深綠色！拿起備用
2. 墨魚滑加入芫荽和蔥碎，再加入芝麻和麻油攪拌均勻
3. 把墨魚滑混合物塗在紫菜上面
4. 熱鍋下游，有墨魚滑的一邊向下，中火煎大約5-6分鐘至墨魚滑變金黃色！然後可以反轉另一邊用細火煎多1分鐘即成！

Here is the translation to English:

**Ingredients:**
- Cuttlefish paste: 200g
- Dried seaweed: 1 piece
- Coriander: 1 bunch
- Spring onion: 1 bunch
- Sesame seeds: 2 tablespoons
- Sesame oil: 1 tablespoon

**Instructions:**
1. Without any oil, pan-fry the seaweed on low heat on both sides until it turns a deep green color! Set aside.
2. Add chopped coriander and spring onion to the squid paste, then add sesame seeds and sesame oil, and mix well.
3. Spread the squid paste mixture on top of the seaweed.
4. Heat the pan, place the side with the squid paste down, and pan-fry on medium heat for about 5-6 minutes until the squid paste turns golden brown! Then, flip it over and pan-fry the other side on low heat for 1 more minute to finish!

Baked Pasta 

Creamy,Cheesy,Crispy and full of flavours,this Baked Pasta is a win win.

Ingredients:
Oil 4 tablespoon 
Carrot 2 (chopped)
Capsicum 1 (chopped)
All Purpose Flour 2 tablespoon 
Milk 3 cups 
Salt to taste 
Black Pepper Powder 1 teaspoon 
Red Chilli Flakes 1/2 to 1 teaspoon according to spice preference 
Pasta 1 packet ( boil according to package instructions)
Mozzarella Cheese as required 

Method:
Add oil in a pan, add chopped vegetables and sauté it.
Move the vegetables on one corner of the pan and add all purpose flour,cook it until it changes its colour then mix it with vegetables.
Add milk, all the spices and cook until it get little thickens.
Off the flame, add boiled pasta, give a mix and transfer it to the baking dish.
Top it with alot of cheese and bake until cheese melts and became crispy and brown.
Enjoy.

由鈦金屬製的抹刀一試愛上，精準與優雅並存，令每道菜的擺盤品更臻完美。 #食物擺盤 是一門藝術，我一直相信，如果一道菜的味道是 100 分，美麗的擺盤能讓它味道加分。畢竟，我們總是先用眼睛品嚐，當食物看起來賞心悅目，大腦就會告訴我們它一定很好吃。🍽️✨

佢仲可刻名呢！沒有什麼比使用專屬自己的工具更特別的了。

Drinking saunf water can have many health benefits, including: 
Digestion
Saunf water can help with digestion by reducing bloating, gas, and indigestion. It can also soothe the stomach lining and relieve heartburn and acidity. 
Oral health
Saunf water can freshen breath and promote oral hygiene by reducing the growth of bacteria. 
Immune system
Saunf water is a good source of vitamin C, which helps boost the production of white blood cells that fight infection. 
Skin health
Saunf water contains antioxidants that can protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne and other skin conditions

Bahan2nya
Ikan Tilapia di gaul garam serbuk kunyit & sedikit perasa.
Goreng kan dalam minyak yang panas sehingga keperangan.
Mori tabukan sedikit tepung gandum dalam minyak panas sebelum menggoreng.

Bahan2 kisar
1 biji bawang besar
3 ulas bawang putih
Cili padi suka pedas
1 pokok akar ketumbar
Kisar kasar

Tumiskan sehingga naik bau wangi bersama sebatang serai diketuk dan kerat dua
Hampir separuh masak, masukkan 3 helai daun limau purut
Masak sampai naik bau wangi tapi tidak terlalu garing
Masukkan 2 sudu makan sos tiram
Sedikit air untuk tambah sambal yang basah
1 sudu makan sos ikan
Masukkan 1 sudu kecil tepung jagung digaul dengan sedikit air.
Masukkan 1 sudu kecil palm sugar, jika tiada masukkan 1 sudu kecil gula
Perasa dengan garam & perasa secukupnya
Akhir sekali taburkan daun ketumbar, matikan api dan perah kan limau nipis.
Gaul dan tuangkan atas ikan tadi.

Selamat mencuba

Mathe Wale Aloo – Quick Recipe

- Heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin, and hing.
- Add green chilli, garlic, and, sauté.
- Add besan, roast till golden.
- Mix in turmeric, red chilli, coriander powder.
- Add boiled mashed potatoes, stir well.
- Pour in buttermilk, simmer for 5-6 mins.
- Add salt, garam masala, pudina powder, Kasuri methi.
- Garnish with coriander leaves.
- Serve hot with puri!

Slow Roast Shoulder of Lamb

Here is a lovely way to cook shoulder of lamb - perfect for Easter Sunday.
A few minutes prep, then you let the oven work its magic and you end up with the most succulent and sweet lamb which will literally be falling off the bone. The salsa verde helps to take it up a notch and also balances the richness of the lamb.
 

 
INGREDIENTS 
1 shoulder of lamb 
4 small onions - sliced 
1 whole garlic bulb - top sliced off
 
To make a jus
1 teaspoon Red currant jelly 
100 ml red wine
 
Salsa verde
1 small bunch coriander 
1/2 small bunch fresh mint
1-2 cloves garlic 
4 anchovies
3-4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
1 tablespoon of red or white wine vinegar 
1-2 teaspoons Dijon mustard
1 teaspoon honey
Salt and pepper 
 
Preheat the oven to 200 degrees c conventional, 180 degrees c fan. 
 
Add onions and garlic to a roasting dish, drizzle with a little olive oil & season. Sit the lamb on top. You want the lamb to fit quite snug. Season with salt & pepper.
 
Cover with foil and cook in the oven for for 30 minutes. Now turn the heat down to 170 degrees c conventional, 150 degrees c fan for 6 hours, but anything up to 8 hours, removing the foil for the last 20 minutes. 
 
Whizz the salsa verde ingredients together and set aside.
 
Set the lamb aside on a warm plate and cover with foil. Spoon as much as the fat from the pan. Add the wine and bring to a simmer on the hob. Squeeze the soft garlic from the skin and mix into the sauce. Add the redcurrant jelly and scrape all of the flavour from the pan. For a smooth jus, then pass through a sieve. How good is this?! 10 minutes, 10 ingredients, under $10 🤯 @chef_mcleod continues to inspire 🍝⁣
⁣
RECIPE⁣
Spaghetti with Peas & Bacon⁣
Budget-friendly dinner, serves 4-6 ($10 Dinners)⁣
⁣
Ingredients:⁣
⁣
🌱 For the Pasta:⁣
⁣
180g + 325g Frozen Peas⁣
5 Cloves Garlic (finely chopped)⁣
1 Bunch Mint (leaves picked)⁣
125ml Water⁣
1 x 400g Bag Spaghetti⁣
Salt (for boiling)⁣
⁣
🥓 For the Sauce:⁣
⁣
480g Streaky Bacon (diced) (substitute with your preferred bacon)⁣
1 Medium Onion (finely diced)⁣
100ml Thickened Cream⁣
100g Parmesan (finely grated)⁣
½ Lemon⁣
Freshly Milled Pepper⁣
⁣
Method:⁣
⁣
1️⃣ Cook the Peas:⁣
⁣
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.⁣
Add 180g of peas and cook for 2 minutes.⁣
Use a slotted spoon to transfer peas to a Braun Hand Blender, then add 2 cloves of garlic, half the mint, and water.⁣
Blend on high speed to a smooth puree, then set aside.⁣
⁣
2️⃣ Cook the Pasta:⁣
⁣
In the same pot, cook the pasta 1-2 minutes less than the package instructions (around 6 minutes).⁣
Reserve 250ml of pasta water before draining.⁣
⁣
3️⃣ Crisp the Bacon:⁣
⁣
While the pasta is boiling, cook the diced bacon in a large fry pan over medium heat until crispy and fat is rendered.⁣
Remove bacon with a slotted spoon and set aside on paper towels.⁣
⁣
4️⃣ Make the Sauce:⁣
⁣
In the same pan, cook the diced onion in the bacon fat until soft (about 5 minutes).⁣
Add the remaining garlic and cook for 1 minute.⁣
Stir in the remaining frozen peas and cook until thawed, then add cream. Bring to a simmer and stir in half of the parmesan.⁣
⁣
5️⃣ Combine:⁣
⁣
Return bacon to the pan and add the cooked pasta.⁣
Gently toss to combine and stir in the pea puree.⁣
Add reserved pasta water to thin out the sauce if needed.⁣
⁣
6️⃣ Finish:⁣
⁣
Spritz with lemon and scatter remaining parmesan and mint.⁣
Season with salt and freshly cracked black pepper to taste.⁣
⁣
Quick, creamy, and full of flavor! The perfect weeknight pasta dish⁣
⁣
💚 Enjoy!⁣
⁣⁣

Recipe ⬇️ Chè Trôi Nước - Sticky Rice Balls in Ginger Syrup & Coconut Milk

Mung Bean Filling
1 bag (14 oz) Mung Beans - Rinse & soak in warm water overnight
Next day, rinse, drain, add to a steamer and steam for 30-45 mins.
Measure out 4 cups of cooked Mung Beans
Blend in a food processor until smooth, add to a bowl
Add 1 Tsp Sea Salt, mix well, taste & adjust as needed
On med heat, add 1/3 cup Vegetables oil to a small sauce pan
Add 3 Tbsp minced Shallots
Stir until a golden brown
Immediately pour the Shallot oil into the bowl of Mung Beans
Mix well, form into 1 tbsp balls, set aside

Dough
Add 1 (16 oz) bag Glutinous Rice Flour into a large mixing bowl
1 Tbsp Rice Flour, mix
Add 1 cup boiling water, mix
Slowly add in 3/4 to 1 cup room temp water and mix until it’s a soft dough 
Cover let rest for 20 minutes

Forming the balls:
Oil hands 
Grab a pinch of dough
Roll into a ball, flatten into a disc
Place mung bean into the center
Carefully push the side dough up to seal
Roll ball in hands for 15 secs to smooth and seal 
Place on a tray lined with plastic wrap

Cooking:
Bring a pot of water to a boil
Add sticky balls 
Stir the balls around immediately to prevent it from sticking to the bottom of pot
Once it floats to the surface, remove and add to a large bowl with room temp water

Ginger Syrup
Bring 4 cups of water to a boil
Add in 3 cups brown sugar or palm sugar
Add sliced or smashed Ginger - (2) 3 inches in length
Let simmer on low for 15 mins
Add in the cooked Glutinous Rice Balls
Turn off heat once it simmer

Coconut Milk
Heat up 2 cans Coconut Milk on med heat
Mix together: 2 Tbsp Sugar, 1 Tsp Salt, 2 Tsp Cornstarch, add to Coconut Milk
Stir well until simmer
Turn off heat

Toasted Sesame Seeds
Toast  1/2 cup Sesame Seeds on med low heat
Stir until a golden brown, remove from heat

Serving
Add Glutinous Rice balls and Ginger Syrup to a serving bowl, drizzle in some Coconut Milk
Sprinkle on Toasted Sesame Seeds
Enjoy!
.

.
.

Vintage-inspired cocktail bar tucked away in the heart of Hell’s Kitchen in New York City 🍸✨

Dutch Fred’s @dutchfreds blends old-school New York grit with a lively, contemporary vibe. The space is full of retro charm, perfect for both a casual night out or a pre-theater stop before catching a Broadway show. 🎭

Their cocktail menu is anything but ordinary, it’s packed with creative signatures that balance nostalgia with innovation. 
You can also get large and small shareable bites here from crispy fish tacos topped with cilantro aioli to classic margherita flatbreads, saucy wings, and my favorite was the crispy chili shrimp with a spicy ginger chili drizzle. Everything is bold, flavorful, and made to pair with your drink.

One of the best deals here is their Freddie’s Happy Meal (yes, it’s an adult twist on the childhood favorite 🤭). 
For $27, you get a house martini or vodka cocktail, plus your choice of beef sliders with fries OR a kale caesar salad if you’re in the mood for something lighter. Available daily from 4–11 PM, it’s a must-try.

DISHES FEATURED IN VIDEO 🌮 Fish Tacos – Fried cod, shredded lettuce, cilantro aioli, pickled jalapeños & onions, micro cilantro 🌮 Chicken Tacos – With a sauce of your choice 🍕 Classic Margherita Flatbread – Mozzarella, tomato, basil & olive oil 🍤 Chili Crisp Shrimp – Crispy bites tossed in a spicy ginger chili sauce

Whether you’re here to sip cocktails with friends, grab a bite before the theater, or just enjoy a lively night out in NYC, Dutch Fred’s is one of those spots that combines history, flavor, and fun all in one.
📍WHERE: 307 W 47th St, between 8th & 9th Avenues

Nothing says the holidays like the sweet, nutty perfection of Pecan Pie! 🥧✨ This All-Natural Holiday Pecan Pie recipe is a cleaner take on a classic favorite—rich, gooey, and made with wholesome ingredients you can feel good about. The best part? The simple list of ingredients.

We baked ours in the Pampered Chef (@pamperedchef) Stone Pie Plate, and the results were pure magic! 🌟 The stoneware ensures even baking for that perfectly golden crust, and its elegant design makes it a showstopper on any holiday table.

Ingredients
1 unbaked 9-inch pie crust
3 eggs, lightly beaten
1 cup toasted halved pecans
1 cup dark maple syrup
½ cup molasses
3 tablespoons melted butter
2 tablespoons white whole wheat flour
1 tablespoon brandy, cognac, or apple juice

Instructions
Preheat oven to 400°F. Place pie shell in pie pan on a baking sheet.

Combine remaining ingredients in a large mixing bowl; stir well to combine. Pour filling into prepared pie shell. Bake until crust is golden and pie has puffed slightly, about 40 to 45 minutes.

Fakeaway Salt & Pepper Chicken, follow @archerkitchen for more easy, high-protein meals!

This is my super quick, high-protein Fakeaway Salt & Pepper Chicken. It’s just as good as your fave takeaway — half the calories and cost too. Let me show you how to make it:

📌 Save this for later — or tag a mate who needs to make this!
 Ingredients: Serves 4 (324 calories | 31g protein per serving) 
* 500g chicken thighs, chopped into bite-size pieces
* 2 bell peppers, sliced
* 1 onion, sliced
* 1 tbsp chopped ginger
* 1 tbsp chopped garlic
* 1 red chilli, sliced
* 2 spring onions, sliced
* 1 packet salt & pepper seasoning (I used @aldiuk )
* 1 tbsp Chinese five spice
* 1 tsp sugar
* 1 tbsp soy sauce
* 1 tsp olive oil
* 1 tbsp cornflour

Method:
1. In a bowl, mix chicken with cornflour, soy sauce, salt and pepper seasoning and a good pinch of salt and pepper. Air fry at 240°C for 10–12 mins until golden and crispy.
2. Meanwhile, heat 1 tsp olive oil in a pan. Add peppers, onion, ginger, garlic, chilli, and spring onions. Season with five spice and sugar, then sauté for 5–10 mins.
3. Stir in the crispy chicken and toss everything together until coated and sizzling.
4. Top with extra spring onions and chilli if you like — then tuck in!

Dieses Rumpsteak mit Chimichurri ist der perfekte Reminder, dass gesunde Ernährung gar nicht bedeutet, ständig das Rad neu erfinden zu müssen. Oft fehlt es nur an Ideen – und manchmal kann es so simpel sein und trotzdem so gut. ✨
Und dieses Gericht glänzt sogar mit ganzen 53 g Eiweiß pro Portion!

Ihr braucht:
• 🥩 Rumpsteak, ca. 230 g
• 🥦 Grüne Bohnen, ca. 100 g
• 🍠 Halbe Süßkartoffel
• 🫒 Olivenöl-Spray
• 🧂 Salz, Pfeffer
• 🌿 Für das Chimichurri:
 – 🌱 Petersilie, ca. 2 EL gehackt
 – 🌶️ Chiliflocken
 – 🫒 1 EL Olivenöl
 – 🍋 Spritzer Zitronensaft
 – 🍇 ½ EL Weißweinessig
 – 🧄 2 Knoblauchzehen, gehackt
 – 🧂 Salz, Pfeffer

So geht’s:
• Die halbe Süßkartoffel längs halbieren, mit etwas Öl einsprühen und mit der Schnittfläche nach unten bei 180 Grad Umluft etwa 30 Minuten backen. Je nach Größe gern öfter prüfen.
• Das Steak etwa 30 Minuten vorher aus dem Kühlschrank nehmen, damit es Raumtemperatur bekommt. Dann salzen und in einer heißen Pfanne von beiden Seiten scharf anbraten.
• Für ein perfektes medium Steak:
 – Beidseitig je 1 bis 2 Minuten kräftig anbraten
 – Hitze reduzieren und je nach Dicke weitere 2 bis 3 Minuten gar ziehen lassen
 – Wichtig: Danach unbedingt 5 bis 7 Minuten ruhen lassen
• Die grünen Bohnen währenddessen in Salzwasser kochen.
• Für das Chimichurri alle Zutaten vermischen und frisch über das aufgeschnittene Steak geben.

Nährwerte pro Portion:
• 643 kcal
• 26 g Kohlenhydrate
• 53 g Eiweiß
• 33,7 g Fett

Tipp: Chimichurri wird noch besser, wenn es 5 bis 10 Minuten durchzieht – so verbinden sich die Aromen perfekt mit dem warmen Steak. 🥩💚

Embrace the flavors of fall with Chef Keda Black’s 🍂 FALL FLAVORED ROAST CHICKEN 🍂! Picture this: tender, juicy chicken infused with hints of thyme, surrounded by sweet, fragrant quince, hearty carrots, and caramelized onions—all simmered in a buttery cider glaze that captures the essence of the season. 🍏🍗🍯 The whole kitchen fills with warmth as this dish roasts slowly, making it perfect for a cozy family dinner on a crisp autumn evening. Paired with creamy mashed potatoes, every bite is a taste of comfort and fall magic. 🍁✨ For more fall-inspired creations, check your local listings and tune into “PARIS BISTRO COOKING with Edward Delling-Williams” on RECIPE.TV! 📺

✨FALL FLAVORED ROAST CHICKEN✨
Serves: 6

Ingredients:
•1 4 lb. chicken
•2-3 large quince
•6 carrots
•3 oz butter, softened
•3 onions
•3 cloves garlic
•3 cups cider
•1 tbsp honey
•salt and freshly milled black pepper
•thyme or oregano
•a small bunch of chives
•about 5-6 sprigs parsley

Instructions:
1. Salt the chicken inside and out, wrap, and refrigerate overnight.
2. Before cooking, bring to room temp, rub with butter, and stuff with thyme.
3. Preheat oven to 355°F. Roast the chicken on each thigh for 20 minutes, then on its belly for 20 minutes, and finally breast-side up for 20 minutes.
4. Lower oven to 270°F and roast for 40 more minutes, basting regularly.
5. Add quince, carrots, onion, and garlic to the dish 1 hour before cooking ends.
6. Once done, remove chicken, quince, and carrots; cover with foil.
7. Soften onions and garlic in 2 tbsp of pan juices on the stovetop, add cider and honey, and return to oven at 355°F for 15 minutes.
8. Reheat everything together for 10 minutes, garnish with chives and parsley, and serve with potatoes.

QUICK BREAKFAST GOALS 😇😇

Best use of leftover besan batter .... lots of veggies and whip up a delicious Besan Handvo! Packed with protein and flavor, this Gujarati favorite is perfect for a quick breakfast or snack 👌👌

Ingredients 🍕
- Leftover besan batter
- 1/2 cup chopped onion
- 1/2 cup grated bottlegourd
- 1-2 chopped green chilies
- 1-inch piece of ginger, chopped
- 1/2 cup chopped carrot
- 1 chopped tomato
- 1/4 cup peas
- Salt, to taste
- 1/2 tsp red chilli powder
- Chopped mint leaves, for garnish
- 1/2 tsp Eno fruit salt (or baking soda)
- Mustard oil, for cooking
- Tempering ingredients:
 - Hing (asafoetida)
 - Jeera (cumin seeds)
 - Yellow mustard seeds
 - Black mustard seeds
 - Curry leaves
 - Black sesame seeds
 - White sesame seeds
 - Chopped coriander leaves

Instructions
1. Prepare the batter: Mix the leftover besan batter with chopped onion, grated bottlegourd, green chilies, ginger, carrot, tomato, peas, salt, chopping mint leaves and red chilli powder.

2. Add Eno fruit salt: Add the Eno fruit salt (or baking soda) to the batter and mix well.

3.Tempering: Heat some mustard oil in a small pan and add hing, jeera, yellow mustard seeds, black mustard seeds, curry leaves, black sesame seeds, and white sesame seeds . Let it splutter. Add chopped coriander leaves. 

4. Add batter: Pour the batter mixture over the temper.
5. Cook : Cover the pan  or dish with a lid and cook on low heat for 5 minutes or until you get a clean wooden stick when inserted.
6.Serve: Serve hot with your favorite chutney or sauce.

Tips and Variations
- Use leftover besan batter to make the recipe more convenient.
- Adjust the amount of spices and chilies to your taste.
- Add other vegetables like zucchini, bell peppers, or cauliflower to the batter.

One Pot Mac and Cheese 🧀🥰

Schnell, einfach und unglaublich lecker 😋
Überzeugt mich immer extrem durch die Cremigkeit & Leichtigkeit des Rezeptes 🥰

Zutaten:

- Cheddar Käse in Scheiben 
- Emmentaler Käse 
- geriebener Käse
- Mozzarella Kugeln
- Nudeln am besten Makkaroni 
- 300 ml Sahne
- 350 ml Wasser 
- Salz
- Pfeffer

Zubereitung:

Nehmt eine Auflaufform. Legt auf den Boden der Form mit den verschiedenen Scheibenkäse Sorten aus, gebt die Nudeln in die Form. Wasser und Sahne hinzu, salzen und pfeffern 😄. 

Den geriebenen Käse und die Mozzarella Kugeln oben drauf und für 40 Min. bei 200 Grad in den Ofen 🧀

Ist die Zeit um, holt die Auflaufform heraus und vermengt nun alles. Sooooo cremig!! 

Unglaublich lecker sagen wir euch 😍

Guten Appetit 😘

#unglaublichlecker #teamzarrellaskitchen

Ingredients
Makes 60 cookies

250grams ghee at room temperature
150 grams of icing sugar
½ kilo of plain flour
¼ cup of atter 
1 cup of raw pistachios 

Method 

In a bowl add the ghee and icing sugar and mix well until fluffy then add the flour and mix until all of the ingredients are combined. Slowly add the atter and once combined turn the dough onto a clean bench and knead gently to form a soft white dough. 
Flatten the dough onto a clean baking tray cover and place it in the fridge to rest for an hour. 

Once it has rested break up the dough into a bowl and begin to break it up and loosen the mixture.  Add ¼ cup of water gradually to help the dough come together, once it feels more like cookie dough, turn it out on the bench and knead it until you have a soft dough.

Preheat the oven to 150 degrees.
Break off 15 gram pieces and roll them into a ball and press with your finger to indent but not flatten, then press the pistachio in firmly. Spread out allowing 2cm around each for the cookie to grow slightly. Bake for 20 mins or until the bottom of the cookies are golden brown.
Remove and allow to cool, store in an airtight container or biscuit tin until ready to serve.

For the Atter

Ingredients

2 cups of sugar
1 cup of water
1 ½ TBS orange blossom water
1 TBS lemon juice

Method

Add sugar and water to a pot and bring to a boil for 5 minutes. Add the orange blossom water and lemon juice and boil for an additional 5 minutes until the mixture begins to thicken. Remove from the heat and use according to the recipe or allow to cool before storing in a jar.

Action finds|| 3 organization tips 📌

Nu ik bezig ben met het organiseren van mijn keuken, wil ik hier graag mee doorgaan. Ik merk dat het me rust geeft, juist omdat ik vaak al druk ben in mijn hoofd. Een opgeruimde omgeving helpt echt! Daarom deel ik graag een paar handige en betaalbare tips met jullie:

1. Doucherek (€2,49)
Ik heb dit rekje heel eenvoudig aan de keukendeur bevestigd. Superhandig om doekjes of andere kleine spullen in op te bergen. Simpel, maar effectief!

2. Opbergdoos met lade (€3,99)
Eerst had ik de kleinere variant meegenomen en nu vond ik bij Action de grotere versie. Perfect voor het organiseren van schoonmaakspullen. Zo heb ik bijna al mijn kleine schoonmaakitems overzichtelijk opgeborgen.

3. Storagebox (€2,99)
Deze box gebruik ik voor de grotere spullen in mijn keukenkast. Je kunt er ook andere schoonmaakmiddelen in kwijt. Het helpt enorm om alles per categorie op te bergen.

Daarnaast heb ik het beige rek in een andere kast geplaatst. Deze keukenkasten zijn nu helemaal klaar! De volgende stap? De kruidenkast aanpakken.

Wat vinden jullie ervan geworden?
Sla dit bericht op voor later en volg me voor meer inspiratie!

Elevate your pasta game with these 3 tips: 

Use pasta water to emulsify your sauce: When you drain your cooked pasta, don’t discard the pasta water. Instead, use a ladle to scoop some of the starchy water into your saucepan. The pasta water helps emulsify the sauce and makes it creamy and flavorful. Just be careful not to add too much water, as it can make your sauce too thin.

Properly salt your pasta water: Adding salt to your pasta water is crucial to infuse the pasta with flavor. As a general rule, use about 1 tablespoon of salt per 4-6 quarts of water. Be sure to salt the water after it comes to a boil and before adding the pasta.

Don’t overcook your pasta: Overcooking pasta can lead to a mushy or rubbery texture. Be sure to follow the cooking time on the package, but also taste the pasta a few minutes before the time is up to ensure it is cooked to your liking. It should be al dente, or firm to the bite.

Follow @beyondtheshot for more tips and weekly recipes

झणझणीत मसाला भाकरी कि मसाला पिझ्झा? नक्की काय नाव द्याल? Masala Bhakari Recipe / Jwarichi Bhakari 
 
#bhakarirecipe #masalabhakarirecipe #ASMRcooking #saritaskitchen #bhakarirecipesmarathi

मसाला भाकरी रेसिपी | Masala Bhakari Recipe
साहित्य | Ingredients
भाकरी करण्यासाठी | For Making Jwarichi Bhakari Recipe
	•	ज्वारीचे पीठ १ कप | Jowar flour 1 cup
	•	पाणी | Water
मसाला भाकरी साठी | For Masla bhakari
	•	भाकरी २ | Bhakri 2
	•	बारीक चिरलेला कांदा १ मोठा | Finely Chopped Onions 1 large
	•	बारीक चिरलेला टोमॅटो १ मोठा | Finely chopped Tomatoes 1 large
	•	बारीक चिरलेली कोथिंबीर पाव कप | Chopped Fresh Coriander  ¼ cup
	•	काळं तिखट २ चमचे | Kala Masala / kanda lasun masla 2 tsp
	•	मीठ चवीनुसार | Salt to taste
	•	तेल २-३ चमचे | Oil 2-3 tsp
	•	बारीक शेव | Nylon Sev 
	•	लिंबाचा रस | Lemon Juice
कृती -
भाकरी करण्यासाठी |
	•	परातीमध्ये ज्वारीचे पीठ घेऊन पाणी घालून मळून घ्या
	•	लगे तसे भाकरीचे पीठ घेऊन चांगले मळून घ्या व उंडा तयार करा
	•	पोळपाटावर ज्वारीचे पीठ भुरभुरून उंडा त्यावर ठेवा, हाताला थोडे पीठ लावून भाकरी सगळीकडून एकसारखी थापा
	•	तवा मध्यम गरम होऊ द्या, पिठाची बाजू वर करून अलगद भाकरी गरम तव्यावर टाका 
	•	गॅस कमी करून, भाकरीला पाणी लावा
	•	भाकरी उलटून घ्या, व दोन्ही बाजूनी भाजून घ्या
मसाला भाकरी चा मसाला -
	1	एका भांड्यात चिरलेला कांदा, टोमॅटो, तिखट, मीठ, कोथींबीर घालून मिसळा
	2	तेल घालून मिसळून घ्या 
मसाला भाकरी साठी |
	•	भाकरी चा पापुद्रा वेगळा करा & मऊ बाजूवर तयार मसाला पाहिजे तेवढा पसरून घ्या
	•	लिंबाचा रस व बारीक शेव पसरून घाला
	•	वरून पापुद्रा ठेवून भाकरी चे चार भाग कटर ने कट करून सर्व्ह करा

This is one of the most useful knife skills to master. So many recipes begin with a chopped onion and if you can do this one step faster, you can make so many meals faster. There’s more than one way to cut an onion, but this is the method I learned in cooking school, and it has served me well over the past two and a half decades. This is how I teach my students to cut an onion in cooking classes, and if you practice, over time it becomes so fast and easy. The reason I love to teach knife skills so much is that it helps you get dinner on the table faster, and isn’t that what we all want? It’s time back in our day! When you read a recipe and the list of ingredients feels a little long, just think how you will feel when you know how to chop everything quickly and efficiently. The prep time will be fast and recipes will take half as long. Dinner will be on the table in no time! Doesn’t that sound nice? I’m Lilie 🤍
I was born and raised in Haiti and moved to the U.S. when I was 15.
I’ve always loved cooking,  long before I had fancy ingredients or a perfectly styled kitchen. Growing up, we didn’t have much, but we made the most of what we had. Food was comfort. It was love. It was how we gathered, even when resources were limited.

That’s where my passion for cooking and hosting began. I learned early on that a meal doesn’t have to be complicated to be meaningful. That a warm table can make people feel seen, welcomed, and at home.

Today, I share elevated but approachable cooking, thoughtful hosting, and homemaking rooted in intention, creating beauty and warmth in everyday moments.

If you love cooking with heart, hosting without pressure, and making a house feel like home, I’m so glad you’re here. 🤍
Follow along! We’re building something cozy together!!

EASY CHICKEN FAJITAS 🍗🌮✨
No stress, no mess—this recipe is perfect for busy nights. Make the fajitas in the oven or air fryer and optional homemade tortillas with beef fat. Here’s how! 👇

Ingredients (for 4):
500g chicken thighs or breasts (sliced)
2 capsicums (1 red and 1 green) (sliced)
1 onion (sliced)
2 tbsp oil
1-2 tbsp Fajita Mix (store bought or homemade: 1 tsp smoked paprika,1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp chili powder, pinch of sugar, oregano)
Salt (to taste)

For the wheat Tortillas:
2 cups plain flour
1 tsp salt
3 tbsp melted beef fat (or butter)
3/4 cup warm water

Method:
For the oven grilled chicken:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C (grill setting) 
2. Toss chicken, capsicums, onion, oil, and spices. 
3. Spread on a tray. 
4. Bake for 20- 25 min, flipping halfway.
 
If making tortillas : 
-Mix tortilla ingredients into a dough, knead, and rest for 10 min. Roll into circles and cook in a dry pan for 1 min/side, steam between tea towels.

To serve:
-Fill tortillas with grilled spiced chicken, sour cream (or yogurt), coriander and your favourite toppings. Enjoy! 🌟

👉 Store-bought tortilla wraps work too, but trust me, homemade hits different! 💯

Could you imagine a more dysfunctional, dated kitchen? Green & brown might’ve been a vibe back in the day but absolutely no place for it in a modern Aussie kitchen makeover. This layout was so strange it had a door to nowhere… so we covered that up real quick & widened the main doorway so the space actually flows into the living area like it always should’ve.

Ripped the whole thing out & started fresh: brand new appliances, grey & white cabinetry & marble benchtops, new lighting & flooring, plus a coat of crisp white paint that instantly made the room feel twice the size. Don't forget that blinging tile splashback. Finished it off with fresh window furnishings because even a newly renovated kitchen deserves a good outfit.

From chaotic throwback to clean, bright & actually functional… get ready for proper family meals again! 🥘✨

Kitchen reno #2 🫣
You’re going to think I am crazy and then you’ll see the big difference between the before and the after and wonder why I didn’t do it sooner.
After years, interior designing is still trial and errors when it’s projects for myself, because I am an eternal perfectionist, when it does not work, I can’t unsee it and it obsesses me until I decide to demolish it all.
 
My mistake here was to have picked two different tiles for the bench and backsplash with the same shape and different finish, one was raw, matt and really rustic and the other glossy and not quite matching colour, one without the other would have worked but not the two together.
 
I decided the change the backsplash thinking I would keep the bench as is, but it was all too rustic for me so the bench tiles had to go too (I managed to salvage a good 75% of them)
And here I was, looking for tiles again, with a specific size and colour. After months, I started to feel desperate and was about to render the bench instead because, of all the samples I ordered nothing was working UNTIL… I found the absolute perfect mosaic tiles over at @abiinteriors while ordering a new kitchen mixer to match my new handles.
Problem solved and kitchen is looking soooo much better, it is not changing anymore, I am set on this combo and obsessed!!
 
Tiles are the Miyako Handmade square mosaic tiles in Maple, but go check their whole range, ABI has the most beautiful selection of tiles and heaps you won’t find anywhere else.

Wok Wok Kitchen — เครื่องแน่นทุกจาน รสเข้มข้น ฟีลข้าวต้มโต้รุ่งแบบพรีเมียมในราคามิตรภาพ!
.
ใครแวะมา ย่านบรรทัดทอง แล้วหิว แนะนำต้องแวะ “Wok Wok Kitchen” คือหนึ่งในร้านข้าวต้มที่ทำให้คำว่า อร่อยฟาด มีอยู่จริง 
.
ร้านนี้เสิร์ฟเมนูโฮมคุกแบบไทยๆ ที่ยกระดับด้วยวัตถุดิบคุณภาพ ปรุงสดใหม่ทุกจาน เครื่องแน่นแบบไม่ต้องขอเพิ่ม ที่สำคัญ…ราคาดีงามจนอยากสั่งทุกอย่างในเมนู!
.
บรรยากาศร้านก็น่ารักคึกคัก ด้วยโทนสดใส เห็นแล้วชวนเดินเข้าไปนั่งสุดๆ ตัวร้านอยู่หัวมุม จุฬาซอย 18 แถวนั้นที่จอดรถก็สะดวกมาก แนะนำให้มาเป็นแก๊งหรือพาครอบครัวมาแชร์ความอร่อย เพราะเมนูเยอะมากจนเลือกไม่ถูก
.
เมนูที่อยากให้ลอง!
•หอยกะพงผัดไข่ — หอยตัวเล็กผัดมากับไข่นุ่ม ๆ รสละมุนกลมกล่อม กินกับข้าวต้มคือจบ
•หมูสับกะปิใส่สะตอ — กลิ่นกะปิหอมตีจมูก รสเข้มข้นถึงใจ ผัดมาแบบถึงเครื่อง คลุกข้าวร้อนๆ คือดีมาก
•สามชั้นทอดน้ำปลา — กรอบนอกนุ่มใน เค็มพอดี กินเปล่าๆก็ฟิน จิ้มน้ำจิ้มคือเด็ด
•ปลากะพงทอดน้ำพริกเนสตุ้ม — เมนูสุดยูนีคที่ทั้งกรอบ หอม และมีรสหวานเค็มเบา ๆ จากเนสตุ้ม แปลกแต่ลงตัว อร่อยเฉยแบยผิดคาด
•มาม่าต้มยำหม้อไฟ หม้อใหญ่สะใจ เครื่องแน่นจุกๆ ทั้งกุ้ง ปลาหมึก หมูกรอบ ไข่ตอก ซดน้ำซุปคือแซ่บจี้ดจ๊าดถึงใจ เผ็ดเปรี้ยวเค็มครบรส จัดจ้านสุดๆ
.
ไฮไลต์อีกอย่างคือ ข้าวสวยและข้าวต้ม “ฟรีรีฟิล” ไม่อั้น! เติมกันได้ทั้งคืน ใครสายกิน สายสตรีทฟู้ด สายโฮมคุก หรือแค่หิวตอนดึก
บอกเลย…ร้านนี้ อิ่มฟิน คุ้มเกินราคาแน่นอน! 
.

Garlicky Tomato Soup + Creamy Goat Cheese Toast = A Warm Hug in a Bowl 🍅🧄🥖

There’s something so comforting about this classic combo. It’s simple, cozy, and packed with flavor—perfect for a lazy evening or a quick, no-fuss dinner fix. 🌟

🍴 Here’s how to make it:
1️⃣ For the Soup:

	•	Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until fragrant.
	•	Add canned tomatoes, season with salt, pepper, and a touch of agave syrup to balance the acidity.
	•	Let it simmer while you prep the toast.

2️⃣ For the Toast:

	•	Toast bread in olive oil and butter.
	•	Generously spread goat cheese on top.
	•	Sprinkle with chili flakes, a little pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil.

✨ Dunk, sip, savor. The perfect pairing for those cozy nights in. 🥣🤍

Whole-Wheat Carrot Walnut Cake (Eggless). 
Spreading festive cheer with a wholesome delight straight from my kitchen — an eggless whole wheat carrot cake loaded with crunchy walnuts. Light, moist, and full of warm flavours… perfect with your chai or coffee. With Christmas just around the corner, enjoy this cozy, heartwarming bake to celebrate the season. 🎄✨
.
.
.

Ingredients
*1/2 cup milk
*1 tsp vinegar
*3/4cup  sugar
*1/2 cup oil
*1/2 tsp vanilla extract
*1 cup wholewheat flour
*1 tsp baking powder
*1/4 tsp baking soda
*1 tsp cinnamon powder
*1.5 cup grated carrot
*1/4 cup chopped walnuts + garnishing
 

Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 40-45 minutes.

Un metodo infallibile per pulire i fondi delle vostre pentole 

In un pentolino unite acqua, gusci di uova e aceto bianco e fate bollire per 10 minuti con coperchio.
Asciugate bene i gusci e frullateli fino ad ottenere una polverina.
Mettetela sopra la parte sporca delle vostre pentole, aggiungete il detersivo e strofinate fino a rimuovere tutto.
Sciacquatele con acqua e le vostre pentole saranno come nuove. 

Adesso non avete più scuse potete lavare le vostre pentole 🙌🏼🙌🏼

Sieve and enjoy Tandoori Chai

✅ 1 Avocado
✅ 1 oz Pumpkin Seeds
✅ Honey or Maple Syrup to Taste

Instructions:
☑️ Toast sliced bread
☑️ Mash avocado in bowl, then spread on toast
☑️ Sprinkle seeds on toast, then pour honey or syrup on top

“Our Kitchen, Our Responsibility! 🍳💨”

Cooking is fun only when safety is in place! 
Follow these **Five Safety Mantras** for your LPG safety: 
1️⃣ Always check the cylinder’s seal and weight during delivery. 
2️⃣ Place the stove on a platform above the cylinder. 
3️⃣ Don’t forget to turn off the regulator when the stove is not in use. 
4️⃣ Get regular inspections and replace the hose every 5 years. 
5️⃣ In case of leakage, switch off the regulator immediately and call 1906 to report it. 

Keep your kitchen safe and double the joy of cooking! ❤️ 

#HamariRasoiHamariZimmedaari #HPGas #HPCL #LPGSurakshaAbhiyaan #LPGSafety #KitchenSafety #StaySafe

🤰 Postpartum Frozen Meal Prep Series - Week 2
🥫 Sweet & sour meatloaf 

📝 What you will need:
1 cup breadcrumbs 
2 lbs meatloaf ground beef mix 
salt & pepper 
2 eggs 
1 onion, grated
garlic 
2x 15 oz cans of tomato sauce 
brown sugar 
4 tbsp apple cider vinegar 
4 tsp mustard 

Combine the fist 6 ingredients + 1 can of tomato sauce. Push tightly into 2 9x5 baking pans. Bake at 375F for about 40 min. 

Meanwhile, combine sauce ingredients and bring to boil in a sauce pan. Pour over meatloaf, bake for another 10 min. Let cook completely before freezing. 

Side ideas: roasted potatoes, mash potatoes, roasted veggies

This is how my daily cooking expedition looks like . Cleaning , washing , marinating , frying , cooking and lastly cleaning , a lot goes on behind the scene . A sneak peak of yesterday’s hard-work . #behindthescenes #mykitchenstories #cooking #dailyvlog #everydaycooking

Costillas de cerdo al horno
Ingredientes para el condimento:
1/2 taza de sal kosher 
1/4 taza de ajo en polvo
1/4 taza de cebolla en polvo 
1/4 taza de orégano 
1/4 taza de azúcar negra 
1/4 taza de paprika 
1 cucharada de pimienta negra 
Procedimiento del sazonador:
1. Mezcle todos los ingredientes en un envase que selle bien y no le entre aire. 

Ingredientes salsa BBQ:
2 cucharadas de salsa bbq regular 
2 cucharadas de ketchup
2 charadas de mostaza
1 cucharada de miel 
1/4 taza de refresco cola
1/4 taza de los líquidos que botan las costillas. 
Procedimiento: 
1. Mezcle bien todos los ingredientes en un envase y mueva hasta que esté todos bien integrados. 

Tiempo y temperatura:
-2 horas en el horno a 350F 
-Luego de que le coloques la salsa bbq, llévalas al horno por 14 minutos adicionales. 
-Antes de cortar las costillas deja que se enfríen un poco por unos 10 minutos.

RECIPE ⬇️ Spinach Shrimp Soup for my daughter since it’s one of her favorite soup and it’s so quick and easy to make. No matter how old she or her older brother gets, I will always make their favorite food when they request for it.

Are you the same way with your adult kids?

This is a great alternative soup to make when you’re craving a warm bowl of Vietnamese soup such as Canh Rau Mòng Tơi, Canh Bí, Bầu or Mướp but you don’t have time to go to the Asian store. Pick up a bag of Spinach from a nearby grocery store and you’re good!

INGREDIENTS:
1 bag - (8 oz) Spinach 
8-10 Large Shrimp (or more if you prefer)
Chicken Bouillon Powder or Soup Seasoning
Sugar
Black Pepper
MSG
Fish Sauce
Rock Sugar
5 cups - water

INSTRUCTIONS:
Rinse the Spinach well, set aside
Peel and deveined the shrimp
Chop and mince the Shrimp until it turns into a paste like consistency

To the shrimp sprinkle on:
1 Tsp Chicken Bouillon Powder
1/2 Tsp Sugar
Dash of Black Pepper
Continue chopping until the seasonings are throughly mixed in with the shrimp

COOKING:
Bring a pot of water to a medium simmer
Form a ball with the shrimp mixture and drop it into the soup

Season the broth with:
1 Tbsp Chicken Bouillon Powder or Soup Seasoning
Dash of MSG
1 Tbsp Fish Sauce
1 Tsp Rock Sugar
Taste and adjust to your liking
Add the Spinach, press down to submerge into the broth
Once the broth simmer, turn off the heat
Serve as is or with rice, enjoy!
.
.
.
.
.
.

From kneading dough to mastering flavors, Chef @risaandithia from @thewheat.id knows that baking is a journey of passion and determination. Follow her recipe to add a touch of artistry to your kitchen. With KitchenAid, the possibilities are endless! 

SALT BREAD

Bahan:
Starter 100g
250g tepung protein tinggi
Gula 30gr
garam 1g
1/4 sendok teh ragi kering instan (sekitar 1,25gr)
1 butir telur (berat bersih sekitar 50g)
Susu cair 110g
30 gram Salted Butter 

Filling :
250gr salted butter 
1/8tsp sea salt

Cara membuat:
Masukkan starter, bahan cair, bahan kering, aduk hingga 90% gluten development 
Masukkan salted butter dan garam. Aduk kembali hingga 100% gluten development 
Proofing hingga double size
Timbang @50gr atau sesuai selera, beri filling salted butter, roll adonan seperti croissant
Last proofing hingga double size
Oles eggwash dan beri topping sea salt
Bake di suhu 180C, 15 menit

Bald steht hier ein Wald-Geburtstag an und diese Blüten - Lollies werden wir da auf jeden Fall auch machen!

Ihr braucht: 
Essbare Blüten, wir haben Primeln, Veilchen und Gänseblümchen genommen
Bonbons, wir verwenden zuckerfreie Zitronenmelisse-Ricola @ricola_ch sowie „Gletscher-Eis-Bonbons“ für die blauen Lollies
Lollipop-Form (im Internet bestellt) 
Kleine Stöckchen (z.Bsp. Hasel)

Blüten zuerst pressen! Sie bleiben dann viel schöner, als wenn ihr die Blüten frisch verwendet! 
Mit der schönen Seite nach unten in die Form legen, Stöckchen platzieren und dann je zwei Bonbons in die Mulde legen.  Im vorgeheizten Backofen bei 180° während 8-9 Minuten schmelzen lassen. Ganz auskühlen lassen, dann vorsichtig aus der Form lösen. Viel Freude!

Let’s normalise getting things fixed, it’s not every time we need to buy something new. Before you bin it or rush to “add to cart,” just ask yourself… can I fix it first?

I’m all for making new purchases, but sometimes, fixing something is the answer we desperately need. And now? My vacuum’s got a new lease on life.

This video is also proof that buying solid, reliable items isn’t a waste of time. My @sharkhomeuk vacuum has served me really well over the past 5 years, and I don’t regret a single penny spent back then or now. Honestly, I feel a little too attached to it. I wasn’t ready to say goodbye just yet, and I’m so happy she’s back like nothing ever happened.

Sharing this because someone out there might not realise you can easily order replacement parts online. Heads-up: the floor nozzle doesn’t come with the soft brush, but that’s not an issue. I just removed the brush from the old one, gave it a clean, and popped it into the new part. Easy fix.

Golden, crispy, and straight from Gujarat’s heart Dakor na Gota. 
In this full recipe video, I bring you the authentic taste of tradition, made simple for your kitchen.

DAKOR NA GOTA RECIPE
CHEF AANAL KOTAK
SERVINGS: 4-5 PEOPLE
PREP TIME: 15-20 MINUTES
COOK TIME: 15 MINUTES
TOTAL TIME: 30-35 MINUTES

INGREDIENTS FOR MASALA:

•	1 Tbsp Cumin Seeds
•	2 Dry Kashmiri Red Chilli
•	1 Cinnamon Stick
•	2 Bay Leaves
•	1 Tsp Black Pepper
•	5-6 Cloves
•	1 Tsp Fennel Seeds
•	1Tsp Sesame Seeds

FOR DAL:

•	1 Cup Chana Dal

FOR OTHER INGREDIENTS:

•	1 Tbsp Green Chilli (Coarsely Ground)
•	1 Tbsp Ginger Paste
•	1 Tbsp Black Pepper (Crushed)
•	1 Tsp Sugar
•	1 Tbsp Dry Coriander Seeds (Crushed)
•	Salt to Taste)
•	1/2 Cup Fresh Fenugreek Leaves (chopped)
•	Milk as required 
•	1 Tsp Cooking Soda
•	1 Tbsp Lemon Juice
•	1 Tbsp Hot Oil
•	Oil 

INSTRUCTIONS:

STEP 1: PREPARE MASALA
•In a non-stick pan, roast cumin seeds, dry red chilli, cinnamon stick, bay leaves, black pepper, cloves, fennel seeds, and sesame seeds.
•Grind roasted masala slightly coarse in a mixer grinder.

STEP 2: PREPARE DAL

•Grind chana dal slightly coarse in a mixer grinder. Not like powder.

STEP 3: MIX INGREDIENTS

•In a mixing bowl, combine ground masala, ground chana dal, green chilli, ginger paste, black pepper, sugar, crushed coriander seeds, salt, and fenugreek leaves.
•	Add milk to prepare batter. Mix well.

STEP 4: FINAL MIX

•Add cooking soda to the batter.
•Pour lemon juice and hot oil over soda.
•Mix properly.

STEP 5: SHAPE AND FRY

•Shape the batter into desired shapes (e.g., small balls or patties).
•Heat oil in a deep-frying pan.
•Fry the shaped batter until golden brown and crispy.

STEP 6: SERVE

•Serve Dakor Na Gota hot with Fried green chilli and curd.

Enjoy your delicious Dakor Na Gota!

Protect Your Skin from Pool Chlorine (Naturally!) 💦

The Problem with Chlorine: What’s Really Going On?

Chlorine is a powerful oxidizing agent. Its job in the pool is to break down bacteria and organic matter through chemical reactions. But once our bodies enter that water, chlorine doesn’t discriminate. It reacts with the natural oils on our skin, disrupting the lipid barrier that helps keep moisture in and irritants out. This can lead to:

Dry, itchy, or flaky skin
Rashes or dermatitis in sensitive individuals
Irritated eyes and nasal passages due to chlorine vapors
Disruption of the skin microbiome and mucous membranes

Children are especially vulnerable. Their skin is thinner, their oil glands less active, and their immune systems still developing. If your child emerges from the pool with red cheeks, itchy skin, or a runny nose that seems to last for days—chlorine might be part of the story.

Before your kids jump into the pool this summer, try this herbal trick:

✨ Coconut oil acts as a protective barrier—rub a thin layer on skin + hair before swimming to help reduce chlorine absorption.

Then, after the fun’s over, use this:

After-Swim Herbal Spray
✨ Neutralizes chlorine
✨ Soothes irritated skin
✨ Smells like summer

Recipe:

1 cup chamomile or calendula tea (cooled)
1 tsp ascorbic acid (vitamin C powder)
1 tbsp aloe vera gel (optional)
👉 Combine and pour into an amber spray bottle. Mist all over after swimming.

If using herbal infusion store in the fridge and remake weekly. If you use just distilled water and ascorbic acid (vit c) it will stay good in your beach / pool bag for weeks.

Why it works: Vitamin C neutralizes chlorine, while herbs like chamomile + calendula calm and heal the skin.

A simple post-swim ritual with deep herbal roots. 🌞💧

The next time you are in Mysore, do check out this restaurant called Turmeric Kitchen, located in Lakshmipuram, within the Hotel Airlines premises. It is an all-vegetarian place that serves homely, delicious food. 

@turmeric_kitchen_mysuru has an extensive menu, that has something for everyone. Different types of chaat, sabzis and roti, Indo-Chinese fare like noodles and fried rice, khichdi and rice varieties - there's a lot to choose from. 

We had dinner at Turmeric Kitchen on our recent trip to Mysore, and had a lovely experience. The food was simply made and really nice. 

Here's what we ate at this restaurant: 
- Lemon Coriander Soup
- Sev Poori 
- Masala Poori Chaat 
- Veg Noodles 
- Veg Momos
- Dal Khichdi

We found the prices to be quite reasonable too. 

Turmeric Kitchen also offers breakfast, which we are hoping to check out on our next visit!

When you’re entertaining, you really don’t want to play bartender and make individual cocktails for all your guests. Make a big batch of these and stash them in your freezer. Shake them up with ice and strain into martini glasses or coupes. 
Espresso Martini
Makes 1 cocktail
 
1 ½-2 ounces vodka (I use Tito’s)
½ ounce coffee liqueur, such as Kahlúa or Tia Maria
1 ounce brewed espresso or cold brew concentrate
½ ounce simple syrup
Optional garnish: coffee beans
 
Brew the coffee and let it cool completely. I use my Nespresso maker, but I also like using store-bought cold brew concentrate. It works well for making big batches of this drink.
 
Add ice to a cocktail shaker, then add the cooled coffee, simple syrup, Kahlua and vodka.
 
Shake very well for a minute or two, then strain into a martini glass. Top with coffee beans and serve.
 
Note: The coffee beans are a traditional topping that represent health, wealth and happiness. For a mocha espresso variation, squeeze about an ounce of chocolate syrup in the base of your martini glass before filling it with the shaken cocktail.
 
Big Batch Version
(makes 6 cocktails)
 
12 ounces or ¾ cup vodka
3 ounces coffee liqueur
6 ounces cold brew concentrate
3 ounces simple syrup
A splash of filtered water

Add the ingredients to a glass container with a tight-fitting lid. Shake well and store in the freezer until ready to serve. If you don’t want to shake these individually over ice, you can just shake the glass container from the freezer and fill the glasses.

In every kitchen, towels and cleaning cloths are used daily for wiping spills, stains, and oil. If not cleaned properly, they can harbor germs, bacteria, and unpleasant odors. It’s essential to have separate cloths for cleaning 🧼 and handling food 🥘 Here’s how to clean them effectively:

Kitchen Cloths:

To hand wash:

⭐️ Boil water, add 2 tbsp detergent and half a cup of borax.
⭐️ Submerge cloths, simmer for 10 - 15 minutes, then let cool. Scrub thoroughly.

To machine wash:

⭐️ Set a high-temperature cycle (60-70°C) and add half a cup of borax with detergent.
⭐️ Pre-soak for 30 minutes if possible.

Microfiber Cloths:

To hand wash:

⭐️ Use lukewarm water and mild detergent (Ezee).
⭐️ Soak for 30 minutes, then gently scrub by hand. Don’t use scrubbers.

To machine wash:

⭐️ Set a low-temperature cycle (20-30°C) and add mild detergent.
⭐️ Pre-soak for 30 minutes if possible.

This care ensures your kitchen textiles remain hygienic and fresh ✨

Open Kitchen or Closed Kitchen🤔
Let’s finally talk about which one actually works best for Indian homes.

🍽️Open Kitchen:
Opens up to your living or dining space, keeping you connected while you cook. It’s great for entertaining and makes your home feel more spacious. But, smells, oil splashes, and kitchen noise can travel everywhere, so it’s not for everyone.

🍴Closed Kitchen:
Enclosed within walls, like most traditional Indian homes. It keeps the mess, smell, and noise contained, letting you cook, fry, and experiment freely. But yes, it can feel a bit cut off from the rest of the house.

✅ For Indian homes and our style of cooking, a semi-open kitchen works best, no doors, maybe a small breakfast counter or serving window, so you get the openness without all the mess.

💬Comment “OPEN” or “CLOSED” which one you like!

How I clean & store strawberries to last longer 🍓

• Rinse strawberries thoroughly in plain water
• Soak in vinegar water for few minutes 
• Then add a tbsp of baking soda
• Rinse again with clean water
• Pat completely dry (very important!)
• Remove the leaves, don’t cut the top stem
• Line a jar with folded tissue paper
• Place strawberries gently inside
• Close lid & store in the fridge

Fresh, clean & longer-lasting strawberries 
Save this for later & share with a berry lover

💡 Don’t borrow money for the following things:

❌ Shopping
Buying clothes, gadgets, or trends using borrowed money is risky. Nawawala na ang value ng binili mo, pero yung utang nandiyan pa rin. If you can’t pay it in cash, better wait.

❌ Vices
Alcohol, gambling, or other vices don’t create value. Ubos na ang pera, may utang ka pa. Borrowing for vices only leads to deeper financial problems.

❌ Traveling
Travel is a luxury, not a necessity. Mas masarap bumiyahe kung wala kang iniisip na utang pag-uwi. Mag-ipon muna bago gumala.

❌ Celebrations
Birthdays, weddings, and holidays are meant to be joyful. Pero hindi dapat ipagdiwang gamit ang utang. The stress of paying it later can ruin the happiness.

👉 Borrow money only for things that add value or generate income, not for temporary happiness.

Ghugni Recipe 

Ingredients:
- 3/4 Cup black chana
- 1 Tsp salt (for cooking and seasoning)
- 2 Cups water
- 2 Tbsp mustard oil
- 1 Tsp cumin seeds
- 3 pcs red chili
- 1 Pinch asafoetida
- 3/4 Cup chopped onion
- 1 Tsp gram flour
- 3/4 Cup chopped tomatoes
- 1 Tsp coriander powder
- 1 Tsp cumin powder
- 1/2 Tsp turmeric powder
- 1 Tsp Kashmiri red chili powder
- 1 Tbsp ginger paste
- 1 Tsp green chili paste
- 1/2 Tsp garam masala
- Chopped green coriander (for garnish)

Method:
- In a pressure cooker, add the black chana, 1 tsp salt, and 2 cups of water. Cover with the lid and cook until 3-4 whistles are heard.

- In a kadhai (wok), heat 2 tbsp mustard oil. Add cumin seeds, red chilies, and a pinch of asafoetida.

- Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn translucent. Then, add 1 tsp gram flour and cook for 2 minutes.

- Add the chopped tomatoes, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tbsp ginger paste, and 1 tsp green chili paste. Mix well and cook the masala mixture until the oil begins to separate.

- Pour in the chana water (from the pressure cooker) and cook the mixture for a few minutes.

- Take a small portion of the boiled chana and blend it into a fine paste using a mixer.

- Add the chana paste to the cooking masala mixture. Stir and cook well for a few minutes.

- Add the remaining boiled black chana along with the water to the kadhai. Cover and cook for an additional 5-10 minutes.

- Finally, add 1/2 tsp garam masala and chopped green coriander. Stir to combine and serve hot, garnished with sev.

Seared in **Coconut Oil** for that deep, tropical richness, then simmered into a velvet sauce of coconut milk, premium fish sauce, and savory soy. 🥥💎

✅ Wilted Spinach for that earthy depth

✨New Lobster Mountain✨🦞
At Fung’s Kitchen in Houston, Texas

Introducing the newest dish at Fung’s Kitchen: The Lobster Mountain.

It’s the first of its kind in Houston and it’s a complete showstopper. 

Created by chef Hoi Fung, when you order the Lobster Mountain, giant live New England lobsters are fished from a tank and presented at the table side before they’re taken in the kitchen and cooked in a wok.

The freshly prepared lobster is then plated to resemble a large mountain, complete with crispy French fries and plenty of crispy crunchy garlic! 

Chef Hoi has created pre-set Lobster Mountain menus for parties 4, 6, and 8 people. Each set menu comes with several other dishes for a complete Lobster Mountain feast. 

The lobster mountain pictured is 15 pounds, but you can order smaller lobster mountains starting at just 6 pounds. I definitely recommend just ordering the lobster menu because you get everything — soup, appetizer, lobster, and more, in a multi-course menu.

🪱February is a great month for getting your beds mulched ready for the season ahead🪱

I like to use well rotted manure, homemade compost and any bags of soil improver I’m fortunate enough to have been gifted 💚

Ben’s Kitchen @benskitchen.sg, the only spot in Singapore serving up this unique fusion of flavors—and trust me, it’s worth the trip.

Their Babi Guling (Traditional Balinese Suckling Pig) is the star of the show. With perfectly crispy skin and juicy, tender meat, it’s no wonder they’re the only restaurant in Singapore where you can try this dish.

But wait, the love doesn’t stop there. I also had their Bakmie Spesial, a bowl of springy al dente noodles topped with roasted pork, BBQ pork, and minced pork—it’s comforting, hearty, and packed with flavor. And let’s talk about the Sate Babi—10 sticks of pork satay grilled to smoky perfection, drizzled with Kecap Manis, and available in both sweet and salty versions. It’s an explosion of Indonesian spices you don’t want to miss.

Located in a cozy spot with friendly service, Ben’s Kitchen is perfect for a casual meal with authentic flavors. Whether you’re here for the iconic suckling pig or the other stellar dishes, you’ll leave with a happy tummy and a new favorite go-to. 

#signaturesfromthekitchen #benskitchen #sp #partipost

Ceviche de Camarones 
Ingredientes:
Camarones cocidos y limpios 
Jugo de limón exprimido 
Pimientos de colores 
Cebolla
Cilantrillo 

Procedimiento:
1.  En un envase coloque los camarones ya limpios y cocidos. 
2. Añádale jugo de limón 100% exprimido hasta cubrir los camarón y mezcle muy bien. 
3. Añada los pimientos de colores y la cebolla cortados en cubitos bien pequeños. 
4. Ahora puede añadir el cilantillo cortado bien pequeño. 
5. Condimenté con sal y pimienta a gusto. 
6. Mezcle todo muy bien. 
7. Dejé en la nevera al menos de 2 a 4 horas y sirva. 
8. Al pasar los días tendrá mejor gusto y podrá disfrutarlo mejor. 

Nota: ese ceviche de camarones es bien refrescante y es ideal para disfrutar en la playa, en la piscina o de paseo con la familia. !Esto es Bello!

Summer berries strudel. Highly recommend growing golden raspberries. Birds have left them alone. So far. Boysenberries are so prolific, I don’t bother netting. Tayberries get netted but that is only an inconvenience for the squirrel.
.
In addition to the berries from my garden, I used strawberries left over from making strawberry cheong. I like the texture. I used some of the strawberry syrup to loosen up the mascarpone.
.
Many of my strudels are spur of the moment. I use what I have on hand. I never know how they’ll turn out. So far they’ve been delicious.

Pan-Fried Armenian Kofta! 🔥 
Get ready to dive into a sensational burst of taste with this mouthwatering recipe that’s as fun to make as it is to devour! Let’s whip up some culinary magic together:

🧅 Half a white onion + handful of parsley + 2T minced garlic in a food processor = Flavor Fusion!
🥩 Combine this mix with 1-1.5 lbs of ground beef & 1t Za’atar, sumac, 1t Aleppo pepper, 1/2t turmeric, and 1/2t cumin.
🥒 The secret ingredient? 2 tbsp of pickle juice for that extra tender touch! 🤫
🌡️ Chill the mix for a few hours to let those flavors mingle.

🏈 Roll into 8-10 mini flat footballs- score!
🍳 Heat up 1 tbsp oil + 1 tbsp butter in a pan.
🍔 Fry each side for 4-5 mins until cooked to perfection.

🤤 The result? Pure bliss! These koftas are melt-in-your-mouth tender and bursting with savory goodness. 😍💫 Don’t forget to sprinkle a dash of love while you cook!

Let’s Make *Ekdum Cheesy Party Snack* 🔥🔥. You’ll Need For Garlic Mixture
-Some Peeled Garlic
-Fry these in 2-3 tbsp Oil till Golden Brown
-Add fried garlic in Blender
-100gm Butter
-3-4 Cheese Cubes
-2-3 Green Chillies
-Blend and Make a paste(dont blend too much)
-1 tbsp Chilli Flakes
-1 tbsp Pizza Seasoning
-1 tbsp Peri Peri -Some Chopepd Coriander
-Mix Well 
-Take Bread Slices and spread this mixture followed by mozzarella cheese
-make all the bread slices
-transfer in a baking tray
-Spread flavoured Butter(add peri peri and pizza seasoning in butter and mix)
-Some Mozzarella Cheese and Coriander 🌿 leaves. -Bake for 20-25 minutes on 210C (Time may vary)
Voila Your Cheesy Party Snack is Ready

Kerala aviyal (Serves 5)
Ingredients
5 cups Mixed vegetables Carrot, yam, Raw banana,drumstick, potato, cucumber, white pumpkin,beans and snake gourd
¼ cup thick curd
3 tbsp coconut oil
2 slit green chili
3 tsp coconut oil
Salt as needed
½ tsp turmeric powder
Curry leaves few
1 cup water

To grind
½ cup coconut
1 tsp cumin seeds
1 tsp red chili powder
2 green chili
Few curry leaves

Instructions
Chop the veggies lengthwise.
In a wide pan add 2 tsp of oil. Slit 2 green chili and layer the vegetables one by one.
Add 1/2 tsp turmeric powder and salt.
Add 1 cup of water and cover this.
Cover this and let it cook in a low flame. Once the steam starts coming out, open the lid and mix it well.
Cover this again and cook. Mix in between.
Grind the coconut, 2 green chili, jeera, red chili powder, and 1/2 tsp turmeric powder into a coarse paste.
Once the veggies are cooked, add this coconut paste to it.
Do not cook the veggies till mushy. It should be cooked but should retain its shape.
Mix well.
Whisk the yogurt and add it to the veggies.
Mix well and switch off the flame.
Add 3 tbsp coconut oil and fresh curry leaves.
Kerala Aviyal is ready to serve.

Notes
1. Do not use sour curd, use fresh thick curds.
2. My mom always adds curd to aviyal just before serving, so that you can avoid getting the sour taste.
3. Do not grind the coconut mixture into a fine paste. Just pulse it twice and add.4. You can add raw mango too, if adding skip the yogurt.
5. Kerala Aviyal pairs well with any South Indian meal and is one of the star item on Onam sadhya menu

Menu today was pumpkin Kuzhambu with @mylaporekitchens puli Kuzhambu powder. I love this Podi because it has more fenugreek than other Podis and I think it works great for Kuzhambu. Rasam. Just regular tomato rasam with @mylaporekitchens rasam Podi. 

Vendakkai roast, which was so so good. I don’t like too much of spice powders for Vendakkai because I feel that masks the natural flavour & sweetness it has. So here at home, it’s mostly just sautéed well, until it roasts, with a pinch of asafoetida, red chilli powder and sambar powder. Salt of course. 

Keerai masiyal which my husband finished. I felt bad that we don’t make it often, given our love for Keerai molakoottal, I have promised him that Keerai masiyal will now be featured once a week 😀! He deserves to get something that he loves so much, right?! 

For lunch Rani has made some paneer pin wheels. Easy. Make rotis. Make a paneer stuffing. Grate paneer, add onions, ginger, green chillies, chopped coriander and a bit of garam masala. Spread it on the roti, roll it tightly, slice, and cook it on both sides on a tawa with some oil. 

I have sadly packed some store bought ketchup. Must work on preservative free ketchup. I feel quite guilty consuming something that practically never goes bad. What does that tell you! A food product that doesn’t go bad for months isn’t food. That’s what it tells you.
